title: Dreamy Greek Burrata Pasta
description: A weeknight Greek dish featuring burrata, with a resinous and pillowy texture, and a shojin-friendly ferment, this recipe is perfect for a quick and delicious dinner
instructions: Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta according to the package instructions until al dente
In a large skillet, heat the olive oil over medium heat and add the minced garlic and cook for 1 minute
Add the chopped sun dried tomatoes, artichoke hearts, and kalamata olives to the skillet and cook for 2 minutes
Stir in the lemon juice and cook for an additional minute
Drain the cooked pasta and add it to the skillet, tossing to combine with the tomato and olive mixture
Cut the burrata cheese into small pieces and add it to the pasta, tossing gently to combine
Season the pasta with salt and pepper to taste
Transfer the pasta to a serving dish and top with chopped fresh parsley
